differential_diagnoses: Streptococcus anginosus group liver abscess/cholangitis with secondary septic thrombophlebitis
Duodenal perforation with retroperitoneal abscess and secondary venous thrombosis
Lemierre’s syndrome-like clinical presentation of streptococcal sepsis
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary emboli
Strongyloidiasis hyperinfection syndrome with bacterial translocation
Primary sclerosing cholangitis with secondary infection
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Obtain follow-up imaging (e.g., dedicated CT or MRI) to evaluate the duodenal-renal soft tissue connection
Consult interventional radiology for image-guided drainage of potential retroperitoneal or liver abscesses
Evaluate for underlying immunodeficiency or malignancy given the constellation of symptoms
Monitor inflammatory markers and repeat blood cultures to assess response to current antibiotic regimen
Maintain anticoagulation and monitor for bleeding risk given thrombocytopenia and potential duodenal ulceration
